title: Homework2
text:
Storing both the pre-treatment and post-treatment readings using the c() function in order to combine the data.

preTreatment <- c(130, 128, 116, 124, 133, 134, 118, 126, 114, 127, 141, 138, 128, 140, 137, 131, 120, 128, 139, 135)

postTreatment <- c(114, 98, 113, 99, 107, 116, 113, 111, 119, 117, 101, 119, 130, 122, 106, 106, 124, 102, 117, 113)

Next, we assign names to these vectors by using the paste() function.

names(preTreatment) <- paste("Subject", 1:20, sep = "_")
names(postTreatment) <- paste("Subject", 1:20, sep = "_")

Now we calculate the difference in the measurements by simply subtracting post treatments by pre treatments, and then calculate the average in the blood pressure change.

differenceInTreatments <- postTreatment - preTreatment
print(differenceInTreatments)

 Subject_1  Subject_2  Subject_3  Subject_4  Subject_5  Subject_6  Subject_7 
       -16        -30         -3        -25        -26        -18         -5 
 Subject_8  Subject_9 Subject_10 Subject_11 Subject_12 Subject_13 Subject_14 
       -15          5        -10        -40        -19          2        -18 
Subject_15 Subject_16 Subject_17 Subject_18 Subject_19 Subject_20 
       -31        -25          4        -26        -22        -22 

### Mean is shown next:
print(mean(differenceInTreatments))

[1] -17


The patients who experienced a decrease in blood pressure after treatment (a positive change) are shown below using the which() function.

positiveChanges <- which(differenceInTreatments > 0, useNames = TRUE)
print(positiveChanges)

 Subject_9 Subject_13 Subject_17 
         9         13         17 





print(mean(positiveChanges))

[1] 13

title: Homework2
text:
Storing both the pre-treatment and post-treatment readings using the c() function in order to combine the data.

preTreatment <- c(130, 128, 116, 124, 133, 134, 118, 126, 114, 127, 141, 138, 128, 140, 137, 131, 120, 128, 139, 135)

postTreatment <- c(114, 98, 113, 99, 107, 116, 113, 111, 119, 117, 101, 119, 130, 122, 106, 106, 124, 102, 117, 113)

Next, we assign names to these vectors by using the paste() function.

names(preTreatment) <- paste("Subject", 1:20, sep = "_")
names(postTreatment) <- paste("Subject", 1:20, sep = "_")

Now we calculate the difference in the measurements by simply subtracting post treatments by pre treatments, and then calculate the average in the blood pressure change.

differenceInTreatments <- postTreatment - preTreatment
print(differenceInTreatments)

 Subject_1  Subject_2  Subject_3  Subject_4  Subject_5  Subject_6  Subject_7 
       -16        -30         -3        -25        -26        -18         -5 
 Subject_8  Subject_9 Subject_10 Subject_11 Subject_12 Subject_13 Subject_14 
       -15          5        -10        -40        -19          2        -18 
Subject_15 Subject_16 Subject_17 Subject_18 Subject_19 Subject_20 
       -31        -25          4        -26        -22        -22 

### Mean is shown next:
print(mean(differenceInTreatments))

[1] -17


The patients who experienced a decrease in blood pressure after treatment (a positive change) are shown below using the which() function.

positiveChanges <- which(differenceInTreatments > 0, useNames = TRUE)
print(positiveChanges)

 Subject_9 Subject_13 Subject_17 
         9         13         17 





print(mean(positiveChanges))

[1] 13
